title: Capstone Scope, Dependency Graph, Milestones, and Risk Control · Evidence Triage
summary: A preserved decision record for capstone scope, dependency graph, milestones, and risk control contains one plausible evidence, scope, design, implementation, evaluation, or ownership defect. Find the first unsupported claim before changing the product. Planned artifact: Repair a changed failure in evidence-ordered capstone delivery system and publish the reproduction, evidence, repair, and regression record. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal d…
objectives: State the bounded product thesis, users, decision, outcomes, exclusions, quality floor, and credential claims in one falsifiable contract.
Order new tasks by prerequisite competence and schedule retrieval of earlier skills before their consequential use.
Create vertical milestones that retire a named risk, deliver an affected-user signal, and produce assessable evidence.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.config:
# capstone-project cumulative independent product evidence portfolio
# Browser labs perform deterministic evidence-contract review only.
# They never contact participants or services, execute commands, deploy, or claim live conformance.
workspace: portfolio
project-id: capstone-project
stakeholder: [replace with affected people and accountable decision owner]
decision: [replace with the decision this evidence changes]
problem-evidence: [replace with observed need, counterevidence, and uncertainty]
accessibility: [replace with keyboard, structure, reflow, status, alternatives, and human review]
security: [replace with threat, authorization, abuse, response, and residual risk]
privacy: [replace with purpose, minimization, retention, access, correction, and deletion]
test-strategy: [replace with behavior, changed case, fault, accessibility, security, and recovery]
release-evidence: [replace with revision, artifact, inventory, provenance, validation, and rollback]
recovery: [replace with backup, clean restore, reconciliation, communication, and ownership]
portfolio-proof: [replace with contribution, limitations, counterexample, and unseen defense]
transfer-boundary: authorized controlled research, toolchain, deployment, and review environments
evidence:

steps.title: Inspect the failure record · Debug Scope Thesis
steps.instruction:
debug case — Component deadlines conceal prerequisite gaps, external waits, integration risk, missing evidence milestones, and no trigger for releasing scope. The debug variation 2 changes the stakeholder, access need, evidence source, constraint, failure, and operating environment. Competency probe: State the bounded product thesis, users, decision, outcomes, exclusions, quality floor, and credential claims in one falsifiable contract.

Inspect the Scope Thesis trace for this debug case. Which record connects a precondition, observed state change, and boundary result?

Evidence target: Inspect the failure record · Debug Scope Thesis.
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for state the bounded product thesis, users, decision, outcomes, exclusions, quality floor, and credential claims in one falsifiable contract. For Inspect the failure record · Debug Scope Thesis, keep Scope Thesis connected to mechanically reviewable stakeholder, affected-user, artifact, changed-case, failure, repair, release, recovery, and transfer decision evidence.

content.text: For Scope Thesis, distinguish a claim from evidence by locating the relevant precondition, trace or invariant, and changed input result.

hints: For Inspect the failure record · Debug Scope Thesis, name the types, ownership, and stop path, Scope Thesis precondition, or invariant that must hold.

At Inspect the failure record · Debug Scope Thesis, trace this concrete input and predict values, errors, blocking, and output before reviewing it: Component deadlines conceal prerequisite gaps, external waits, integration risk, missing evidence milestones, and no trigger for releasing scope. The debug variation 2 changes the stakeholder, access need, evidence source, constraint, failure, and operating environment. Competency probe: State the bounded product thesis, users, decision, outcomes, exclusions, quality floor, and credential claims in one falsifiable contract.

For Inspect the failure record · Debug Scope Thesis, challenge the documented misconception with runtime, failure, race, or toolchain evidence: A flexible broad vision is better than a bounded capstone thesis.

stimulus.title: Scope Thesis case record
stimulus.caption: Three excerpts from a learner evidence packet; only one connects a claim to a reproducible check.

lines.label: assumption
lines.text: Component deadlines conceal prerequisite gaps, external waits, integration risk, missing evidence milestones, and no trigger for releasing scope. The debug variation 2 changes the stakeholder, access need, evidence source, constraint, failure, and operating environment. Competency probe: State the bounded product thesis, users, decision, outcomes, exclusions, quality floor, and credential claims in one falsifiable contract.

lines.label: risk
lines.text: A flexible broad vision is better than a bounded capstone thesis.

lines.label: evidence
lines.text: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: State the bounded product thesis, users, decision, outcomes, exclusions, quality floor, and credential claims in one falsifiable contract. Artifact under review: evidence-ordered capstone delivery system.

options.text: Apply the visible technique immediately, omit assumptions, and treat one successful output as complete proof.
The record names the assumption, applies Scope Thesis, and reports a changed-case check tied to the stakeholder decision.
The record says the result looks right but supplies no quantity, trace, condition, or counterexample. That claim does not survive the inspect the failure record evidence boundary.

steps.title: Implement the cause-level repair · Debug Progress Evidence
steps.instruction:

steps.why: Building a runnable or mechanically reviewable artifact turns Progress Evidence into observable performance evidence. content.code:
capstone-progress-evidence-1twxk6:
  verify: [replace with a deterministic check]
  failure: [replace with the rejected condition]
  repair: [replace with a local reproduction and correction path]
content.caption: Shape of the required increment; replace generic language with the current stakeholder case.

steps.title: Publish the incident record · Debug Progress Evidence
steps.instruction:
Write the debug evidence note for Progress Evidence: explain the governing condition, one failure, and how this concrete case was verified — Component deadlines conceal prerequisite gaps, external waits, integration risk, missing evidence milestones, and no trigger for releasing scope. The debug variation 5 changes the stakeholder, access need, evidence source, constraint, failure, and operating environment. Competency probe: Track accepted evidence, changed assumptions, blocked claims, forecast, decision latency, quality debt, and residual risk rather than percent complete.

Evidence target: Publish the incident record · Debug Progress Evidence.
steps.why: Retrieval and explanation expose gaps that recognition questions can hide. For Publish the incident record · Debug Progress Evidence, keep Progress Evidence connected to mechanically reviewable stakeholder, affected-user, artifact, changed-case, failure, repair, release, recovery, and transfer decision evidence.

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Track accepted evidence, changed assumptions, blocked claims, forecast, decision latency, quality debt, and residual risk rather than percent complete.
